name: University of Cambridge
notes: Cambridge students apply to Engineering (H100) and specialise in aerothermal and aerospace engineering from Year 3. ENGAA required; interview at college. Cambridge's aerospace group includes the Whittle Laboratory, world-leading in gas turbine aerodynamics. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: Imperial College London
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Imperial Aeronautics is one of the world's top aerospace engineering departments. World-class in aerodynamics, structures and propulsion. Major recruiters include Rolls-Royce, BAE Systems, Airbus and MBDA. A* in Maths expected; Physics required.

name: University of Bristol
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Bristol is arguably the UK's premier aerospace engineering university outside Oxbridge-Imperial. Airbus UK's main UK engineering centre is in Filton (3 miles from campus), providing exceptional placement and career opportunities. Strong aerodynamics, composites and avionics research. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Southampton
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Southampton's aerospace programme is distinguished by its astronautics component and world-leading aerodynamics research. The university operates its own supersonic wind tunnels. Strong satellite engineering and UAV research groups. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Bath
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Bath Aerospace Engineering consistently achieves excellent placement and graduate employment outcomes. Placement (Semester 5) with employers including Airbus, Rolls-Royce, BAE Systems and Leonardo. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Manchester
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Manchester Aerospace benefits from the National Aerospace Technology Exploitation Programme (NATEP) and close industry links. Strong in computational fluid dynamics and composites research. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: Loughborough University
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Loughborough Aeronautical Engineering is one of the UK's highest rated. Exceptional placement culture with aerospace firms. The aeronautics department operates the Loughborough University Wind Tunnel Facility. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Sheffield
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Sheffield's AMRC (Advanced Manufacturing Research Centre) with Boeing is a world-class aerospace manufacturing research facility. Strong composites, metallic structures and manufacturing engineering links. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: Queen's University Belfast
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Queen's Belfast has a well-regarded aerospace engineering school. Belfast's aerospace cluster includes Bombardier/Spirit AeroSystems — one of the world's largest aircraft component manufacturers — providing exceptional local industry links. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Glasgow
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Glasgow Aerospace Engineering has strong aerodynamics, flight mechanics and propulsion research. Scottish 4-year BEng or 5-year MEng. Industry links with BAE Systems (Warton, Lancashire) and Leonardo (Edinburgh). Mathematics and Physics required.
